_meta.figure: Paper B, Figure fig8_int_vs_ternary (\label{fig:intvster}): integer K-quants survive; uniform ternary K2 collapses
_meta.what: Integer llama.cpp K-quant accuracy points used as the survival baseline against uniform ternary K2/K3.
_meta.harness: EleutherAI lm-eval HFLM (same harness as the ternary sweep); quantize->dequant->accuracy, device-independent
_meta.bpw_definition: real GGUF file-based storage of the quantized weights (packed), not the information-theoretic entropy bound
_meta.provenance_caveat: The per-run lm-eval result JSONs for these integer K-quant points were NOT retained -- pods were torn down after the run per the project's capture->delete policy, and the values were transcribed at capture time into sasori/bench/plot_pareto_int_vs_ternary.py (committed 2026-06-30). They are reproduced verbatim in this file so the figure loads from a single auditable data artifact rather than from an inline code literal. The ternary side of the SAME figure loads from a retained artifact: experiments/ternary-quantization/data/overnight/clean_sweep_2026-06-30.json.
_meta.independent_support: The qualitative claim the figure makes -- a uniform-ternary K2 reasoning cliff that mixed-precision integer K-quants do NOT reproduce at comparable bpw -- is independently corroborated by the retained RTN int3 control (results/runpod-runs) and by the ternary K2/K3 sweep, both of which are backed by committed JSON. This figure is a positioning/context plot, not a headline result; the headline (the K-lever, fig1) rests on the retained sweep.
